parameters.name: dat
parameters.label: DAT Table
parameters.description: Uses a to specify the target and reference objects to use. The first column will be the target objects while the second column will be the reference objects. No headers are used.

parameters.name: target
parameters.label: Target Object
parameters.description: The object that is being compared to the position of the reference object. The Target Object can be expressed as a text string. This can be useful when the object name needs to be a variable - it allo

parameters.name: reference
parameters.label: Reference Object
parameters.description: The object that acts as the origin of the comparison. The Object can be expressed as a text string.

parameters.name: swaptargetreference
parameters.label: Swap Target/Reference
parameters.description: Swap the objects defined above in the Target Object and Object parameters.

parameters.name: compute
parameters.label: Compute
parameters.description: Specify the information to output from the objects as described in the parameters below. Except for 'measurements', these match the standard transform formats as described by the .

parameters.name: translate
parameters.label: Position
parameters.description: The displacement from the reference object to the target object.

parameters.name: rotate
parameters.label: Rotation
parameters.description: The orientation difference from the reference object to the target object.

parameters.name: scale
parameters.label: Scale
parameters.description: The scale difference from reference object to the target object.

parameters.name: quat
parameters.label: Quaternion
parameters.description: The quaternion from reference object to the target object.

parameters.name: bear
parameters.label: Bearing
parameters.description: The rotation necessary for the reference object to be facing the target object.

parameters.name: singlebear
parameters.label: Single Bearing Angle
parameters.description: An angle representing where the target object is relative to the reference object. Zero degrees is directly in front, 90 degrees is beside and 180 degrees is behind.

parameters.name: distance
parameters.label: Distance
parameters.description: The distance between the two objects.

parameters.name: invsqr
parameters.label: Inverse Square Distance
parameters.description: The inverse squared distance between the two objects, useful for modeling electric forces, audio dropoff and gravity.

parameters.name: xord
parameters.label: Transform Order
parameters.description: The transform order to use for Rotation, Scale, Transform, Bearing, or Single Bearing Angle modes.

parameters.name: rord
parameters.label: Rotate Order
parameters.description: The rotation order to use for Rotation, Scale, Transform, Bearing, or Single Bearing Angle modes.

parameters.name: includeorderchans
parameters.label: Include Order Channels
parameters.description: Turn on to include channels for Transform Order and Rotate Order.

parameters.name: bearingref
parameters.label: Bearing Reference
parameters.description: Bearing requires a direction to use as a reference base.

parameters.name: tscopex
parameters.label: Point Scope X
parameters.description: When one of the optional point inputs is connected, this determines which channels represent X, Y and Z.

parameters.name: tscopey
parameters.label: Point Scope Y
parameters.description: When one of the optional point inputs is connected, this determines which channels represent X, Y and Z.

parameters.name: tscopez
parameters.label: Point Scope Z
parameters.description: When one of the optional point inputs is connected, this determines which channels represent X, Y and Z.

parameters.name: appendattribs
parameters.label: Append Attributes
parameters.description: Adds a rotate attribute to any rotation channels the Object creates.

parameters.name: smoothrotate
parameters.label: Smooth Rotation
parameters.description: When on outputs a smooth rotation curve without graphical jumps at 0, 90, etc.

parameters.name: nameformat
parameters.label: Channel Names
parameters.description: Sets how the created channels are named.

parameters.name: outputrange
parameters.label: Output Range
parameters.description: The start and end time of the desired interval of the object path.

parameters.name: cookpast
parameters.label: Cook Past Values (slow)
parameters.description: If the project has skipped one or more frames, this will attempt to cook it's inputs at multiple previous frames to avoid discontinuities in it's calculations.

parameters.name: start
parameters.label: Start
parameters.description: The start time of the desired interval of the object path.

parameters.name: startunit
parameters.label: Start Unit
parameters.description: Select the units to use for this parameter, Samples, Frames, or Seconds.

parameters.name: end
parameters.label: End
parameters.description: The end time of the desired interval of the object path.

parameters.name: endunit
parameters.label: End Unit
parameters.description: Select the units to use for this parameter, Samples, Frames, or Seconds.

parameters.name: left
parameters.label: Extend Left
parameters.description: The extend condition before the interval. They are:

parameters.name: right
parameters.label: Extend Right
parameters.description: Extend condition after the interval. Same options as Extend Left.

parameters.name: defval
parameters.label: Default Value
parameters.description: The value used for the Default Value extend condition.

parameters.name: timeslice
parameters.label: Time Slice
parameters.description: Turning this on forces the channels to be " ".  A is the time between the last cook frame and the current cook frame.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.label: Scope
parameters.description: To determine which channels get affected, some CHOPs use a string on the Common page.

parameters.name: srselect
parameters.label: Sample Rate Match
parameters.description: Handle cases where multiple input CHOPs' sample rates are different. When Resampling occurs, the curves are interpolated according to the Interpolation Method Option, or "Linear" if the Interpolate Op

parameters.name: exportmethod
parameters.label: Export Method
parameters.description: This will determine how to connect the channel to the parameter. Refer to the article for more information.

parameters.name: autoexportroot
parameters.label: Export Root
parameters.description: This path points to the root node where all of the paths that exporting by are relative to.

parameters.name: exporttable
parameters.label: Export Table
parameters.description: The used to hold the export information when using the Table Methods (See above).

parameters.name: bearingz
parameters.label: Bearingz
parameters.description: An arbitrary base direction for the bearing calculation.

examples.label: Configure Time Slice and Export Method
examples.code:
n = op('object1')
n.par.timeslice = True
n.par.exportmethod = 'datname'
n.par.compute = 'measure'
examples.description: Common settings from 31 real Derivative examples
